query: Which top 3 entities had the most number of filings in FERC's rule making proceedings on large load interconnection in December 2025?
final_answer:
Based on my analysis of all FERC eLibrary filings in the **RM26-4** docket ("Interconnection of Large Loads to the Interstate Transmission System") during December 2025, here are the results:

---

## Top 3 Entities by Number of Filings in RM26-4 (December 2025)

| Rank | Entity | # of Filings | Filing Details |
|------|--------|-------------|----------------|
| **1 (tie)** | **PJM Interconnection, L.L.C.** | **2** | (1) Reply Comments to ANOPR – 12/11/2025; (2) Request to Update Service List – 12/29/2025 |
| **1 (tie)** | **Switch, Ltd.** | **2** | (1) Reply Comments – 12/05/2025; (2) Motion to Intervene – 12/05/2025 |
| **3 (tied among ~48 entities)** | *Many entities tied at 1 filing each* | **1** | Single reply comment or comment filing |

---

### Key Observations

- **The vast majority of entities filed exactly once** during December 2025 in the RM26-4 proceeding. The bulk of filings (approximately 40+) were Reply Comments submitted on **12/05/2025**, the deadline for reply comments on the ANOPR.
- **Only two entities filed more than one document**: PJM Interconnection (2 filings) and Switch, Ltd. (2 filings).
- There is **no clear "top 3"** because the third-most-frequent filers are all tied at 1 filing each. This is a proceeding where a large number of diverse stakeholders each submitted a single set of reply comments.

**Source:** FERC eLibrary AdvancedSearch API, searching docket RM26-4-000 for filings dated 12/01/2025–12/31/2025 (50 results returned per page; data analyzed from `/work/ferc_search_20260615_183619.csv`).
